rulings.Case Title: DAVID BECK VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDENTS(B*P) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR WEDNESDAY, JANUARY 16, 2013 IN DEPARTMENT 503 AT 9:30 A.M., LINE 3, Defendant Todd Shipyards Corp.¿s motion for summary judgment is denied and its alternate motion for summary adjudication is off calendar. As to the causation issue, defendant sustained its initial burden. Mr. Hixson¿s declaration creates a triable issue whether Mr. Beck was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials (insulation) attributable to defendant. As to defendant¿s argument based on Privette v. Superior Court and its progeny, defendant failed to sustain its burden of demonstrating that even if plaintiff cannot demonstrate that an exception to Privette applies, plaintiff¿s claim still fails. Plaintiff represents that he did not allege causes of action for strict liability or false representation against defendant and those were the only causes of action defendant sought to have summarily adjudicated. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45am. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: DAVID BECK VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDENTS(B*P) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR WEDNESDAY, JANUARY 16, 2013 IN DEPARTMENT 503 AT 9:30 A.M., LINE 3, Defendant Anderson, Rowe & Buckley, Inc.¿s motion for summary judgment is denied and its alternate motion for summary adjudication is denied as to issue 3 (premises owner/contractor liability) and off calendar as to issues 1 and 2 (negligence and strict liability). Defendant failed to sustain its burden of demonstrating that the sophisticated user defense applies to bar plaintiffs¿ claims against it. Plaintiff has dismissed his negligence and strict liability causes of action. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45am. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)
